report.name: IT Help Desk Tickets - Problem Category Analysis

highlightsData.title: Poor Category Definition
highlightsData.content: The 'Other' category represents 39.9% of all tickets, the single largest category. This indicates a fundamental failure in ticket categorization and prevents meaningful root cause analysis. Without proper categorization, improvement efforts cannot be effectively targeted.

attachments.name: ticket_categorization_problem.jpg
attachments.description: Example of poor ticket categorization system

highlightsData.title: Vague Problem Descriptions
highlightsData.content: Categories like 'Software Issues' and 'Hardware Problems' are too broad to enable actionable improvements. Is it Microsoft Office? Custom applications? Laptops? Printers? Without specificity, resources cannot be properly allocated to address root causes.

attachments.name: vague_ticket_descriptions.jpg
attachments.description: Examples of unhelpfully vague ticket descriptions

actionItems.title: Redesign Ticket Categorization System
actionItems.description: Create detailed category hierarchy with specific problem types. Analyze 'Other' category tickets to identify proper classifications. Train help desk staff on correct categorization.

actionItems.title: Implement Self-Service Password Reset
actionItems.description: Deploy automated password reset system to reduce help desk load for most common ticket type.

actionItems.title: Conduct Proper Pareto Analysis Training
actionItems.description: Train IT staff on proper data collection and categorization for meaningful Pareto analysis. Include examples of good vs. poor categorization.
